MINI MINUTES: Appearance by Michael John Guglielmino, Esq. for Plaintiff. Matter is set for Case Management Conference on Jul-30-2014 at 10:30 AM in Department 610. JUDGE CYNTHIA M. LEE; Clerk KCox; Proceedings not reported. (610)

CASE MANAGEMENT CONFERENCE
Off Calendar Apr-15-2014 Set For Order To Show Cause On Jun-17-14 At 10:30 A.M. In Dept 610

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
Off Calendar Jan-07-2014 Text Ruling
MINI MINUTES: Appearances: MICHAEL GUGLIELMINO, ESQ., 415.221.7174, FOR PLAINTIFF ZEIDAN.
MATTHEW GLUCK, ESQ., 415.226.1173, FOR DEFENDANTS MURPHY AND JUNQUIERA.
ROBERT GARCIA, ESQ., 415.648.3062, FOR DEFENDANT STEFANO.

RULING: THE COURT VACATES ITS TENTATIVE RULING AND HEARS ARGUMENT ON THE MERITS. The preliminary injunction is granted in part and denied in part. Plaintiff has not demonstrated that sufficient grounds exist for this preliminary injunction with regards to defendant Mr. Stefano. The evidence before the court is that the transaction to sell the business to Ms. Murphy and Mr. Jim Kara progressed and the Court finds that there is no escrow account and there are no funds yet to be paid by Ms. Murphy and Mr. Jim Kara to Mr. Stefano. Thus the relief sought by the order to show cause in paragraphs C, D, E and F are inapplicable to the evidence before the court. The ruling with regard to Mr. Stefano is without prejudice to a future application for any professional remedy whether this or any other provisional remedy and without prejudice to any post judgment enforcement remedies that Plaintiff may wish to take to enforce the underlying judgment he has against Mr. Stefano. As to the relief sought against Ms. Murphy, Mr. June Kara and Bernal Heights pizza in paragraph A of the order to show causes, Counsel for plaintiff and counsel for Ms. Murphy and Mr. Jim Kara are in agreement that a preliminary injunction may issue as to Ms. Murphy, Mr. June Kara and Bernal Heights pizzeria to the limited following extent: They are prohibited from selling, transferring, conveying or otherwise disposing of any of the property of the business including the business property and equipment located at with 5930th Street San Francisco, California 94110, except in the ordinary course of business. As to paragraph B in the order to show cause regarding the liquor license, plaintiff has withdrawn that request so it will be denied. Plaintiff is required to post a $10,000 bond. (Prevailing party to prepare a form of order.) Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Lesley Fiscella, Reporter: Tony Vaughn, CSR #6185

Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order
Off Calendar Dec-23-2013 Continued To Jan-07-14 At 09:30 A.M. In Dept 302
MINI MINUTES:

No appearance on this matter. The Court adopted its tentative ruling.
Order To Show Cause Re: Preliminary Injunction And Temporary Restraining Order is continued to JAN-07-2014 per ex parte order filed on DEC-17-2013. Judge: Marla J. Miller, Clerk: Cynthia Herbert; Court Reporter: Anthony C. Vaughn, CSR # 6185 =302/MJM
